# CI/CD Standards

## hyperi-ci Commands

```bash
uv tool install hyperi-ci
hyperi-ci check              # MANDATORY before every push
hyperi-ci check --quick      # Quality only
hyperi-ci check --full       # Quality + test + build
hyperi-ci init               # Scaffold .hyperi-ci.yaml, Makefile, workflow, .releaserc.yaml, .githooks/
git config core.hooksPath .githooks  # Activate commit hook
```

| Task | Command |
|------|---------|
| Pre-push check | `hyperi-ci check` / `make check` |
| Quality only | `hyperi-ci check --quick` |
| Full local CI | `hyperi-ci check --full` / `make ci` |
| **Push** | **`hyperi-ci push`** (NEVER bare `git push`) |
| Push + auto-release | `hyperi-ci push --release` |
| Push, skip CI | `hyperi-ci push --no-ci` |
| Trigger CI run | `hyperi-ci trigger` |
| Watch CI | `hyperi-ci watch` |
| Failed logs | `hyperi-ci logs --failed` |
| List unpublished tags | `hyperi-ci release --list` |
| Publish a version | `hyperi-ci release v1.3.0` |
| Validate commit msg | `echo "fix: something" \| hyperi-ci check-commit` |
| List commit types | `hyperi-ci check-commit --list` |
| Upgrade | `hyperi-ci upgrade` |
| Run stages directly | `hyperi-ci run quality\|test\|build` |

## Prefer hyperi-ci Over Native Tools

When hyperi-ci is installed, it wraps common `gh` and `git` operations with
project-aware logic (correct workflow, auto-detection, structured output).
**Always prefer the wrapper over the native tool.**

| âťŚ Native tool | âś… hyperi-ci | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `git push` | `hyperi-ci push` | Runs pre-push checks, rebases for semantic-release, supports `--release`/`--no-ci` |
| `gh run watch` | `hyperi-ci watch` | Auto-detects latest run on current branch |
| `gh run list` | `hyperi-ci watch` | Same â€” finds the latest CI run |
| `gh run view --log-failed` | `hyperi-ci logs --failed` | Filter by `--job`, `--step`, `--grep`, `--tail` |
| `gh workflow run ci.yml` | `hyperi-ci trigger` | Resolves workflow from config, supports `--watch` |
| `gh release create/delete/upload` | `hyperi-ci release <tag>` | Full publish pipeline (build, GH Release, registries, R2) |

**Read-only commands remain native:** `gh release view`, `gh run list --json` (scripting),
`gh auth status`, `git status`/`log`/`diff`/`show`, etc.

**Escape hatch:** Prefix with `HYPERCI_ALLOW_NATIVE=1` for one-off native tool use
(e.g. diagnosing a hyperi-ci bug). Example: `HYPERCI_ALLOW_NATIVE=1 gh run watch 12345`.

A PreToolUse hook in hyperi-ai enforces these redirects when hyperi-ci is on PATH.
If hyperi-ci is not installed, the hook is inert â€” native tools work as normal.

## Versioning

- Single branch: `main`. No release branches. No prerelease suffixes (`-dev.8`).
- `semantic-release` determines version, updates `VERSION` + manifest, commits, creates git tag.
- **Nothing is published automatically.** Developer explicitly runs `hyperi-ci release v1.3.0`.
- Skipped versions are normal â€” not every tag needs publishing.
- After push, semantic-release pushes a version commit back â†’ always `git pull --rebase origin main` before next push.

**Never touch manually:** `VERSION`, `CHANGELOG.md`, version fields in `Cargo.toml`/`pyproject.toml`/`package.json`, git tags.

### Major Version Policy (Torvalds Two-Hands Rule)

When the minor version exceeds 20, bump to the next major. Humans count on
fingers and toes â€” version numbers past 20 are a smell that a major bump was
overdue. This is a version hygiene rule, not a semver override.

**Trigger:** Minor reaches 20 AND at least one genuine `BREAKING CHANGE:` exists
in the accumulated commits since the last major. Do NOT fabricate breaking changes
just to bump â€” wait for a real API change (signature change, removed public type,
behaviour change that requires consumer code updates).

**How:** Add a commit with `BREAKING CHANGE:` in the footer describing the real
breaking change. Semantic-release handles the rest.

**Example:** `hyperi-rustlib` graduated from v1.22 to v2.0.0 when
`DfeMetrics::register()` changed signature (real breaking change) and the minor
had exceeded 20 (hygiene trigger).

## Publishing Channels

Set in `.hyperi-ci.yaml` under `publish.channel`:

| Channel | GH Release | R2 Path | Registries |
|---------|-----------|---------|------------|
| `spike` | prerelease | `/{project}/spike/v1.3.0/` | Skipped |
| `alpha` | prerelease | `/{project}/alpha/v1.3.0/` | Skipped |
| `beta` | prerelease | `/{project}/beta/v1.3.0/` | Skipped |
| `release` | GA | `/{project}/v1.3.0/` | Published (PyPI, crates.io) |

Graduate by changing one line in `.hyperi-ci.yaml`. No code changes.

## Commit Message Format

```
<type>: <description>
<type>(scope): <description>
```

- 3-100 chars, lowercase start, imperative mood
- Breaking changes: `BREAKING CHANGE:` in body (never automatic)
- Enforced by `.githooks/commit-msg` + CI quality stage

**Version bump types:**

| Type | Bump |
|------|------|
| `feat:` | MINOR (use sparingly) |
| `fix:` | PATCH (default choice) |
| `perf:` | PATCH |
| `hotfix:` | PATCH |
| `security:` / `sec:` | PATCH |

**No bump:** `docs`, `test`, `refactor`, `style`, `build`, `ci`, `chore`, `deps`, `revert`, `wip`, `cleanup`, `data`, `debt`, `design`, `infra`, `meta`, `ops`, `review`, `spike`, `ui`

| âťŚ Don't | âś… Do | Why |
|----------|-------|-----|
| `updated the config parser` | `fix: update the config parser` | Must start with type prefix |
| `Fix: something` | `fix: something` | Lowercase type and description |
| `feat: x` for every change | `fix: x` for most changes | `feat` = genuinely new user-facing feature |

## Semantic Release Config

`.releaserc.yaml` generated by `hyperi-ci init`.

- `branches: [main]` only
- No `@semantic-release/github` plugin â€” GH Release created by publish step
- `prepareCmd` updates VERSION + language manifest
- `@semantic-release/git` commits with `[skip ci]`

## Workflow Architecture

Consumer projects use a thin `ci.yml` calling the reusable workflow:

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI
on:
  push: { branches: ["**"] }
  pull_request: { branches: [main] }
  workflow_dispatch:
jobs:
  ci:
    uses: hyperi-io/hyperi-ci/.github/workflows/rust-ci.yml@main
    with: { publish-target: both }
    secrets: inherit
```

- **Push to main:** `quality â†’ test â†’ build (amd64 only) â†’ semantic-release`
- **Dispatch (publish):** `checkout tag â†’ quality â†’ test â†’ build (amd64+arm64) â†’ publish`
- Publish pipeline is standalone from the tag â€” no shared artifacts from main push.

## Configuration

```yaml
# .hyperi-ci.yaml
language: rust          # auto-detected if omitted
publish:
  enabled: true
  target: both          # internal | oss | both
  channel: release      # spike | alpha | beta | release
build:
  strategies: [native]
  rust:
    targets: [x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu]
quality:
  enabled: true
  gitleaks: blocking
```

**Config cascade (highest wins):** CLI flags â†’ `HYPERCI_*` env vars â†’ `.hyperi-ci.yaml` â†’ `config/defaults.yaml` â†’ hardcoded
